metadata.name: Magnifying Glass Investigate creates Clue token, Clue activated to draw a card
metadata.description: p1 has Magnifying Glass already on the battlefield untapped, with 5 colorless mana available. p1 activates Magnifying Glass's Investigate ability ({3}, {T}) — paying {3} generic and tapping the artifact. The ability resolves and creates a colorless Clue token on p1's battlefield (CR 701.16a: Investigate means 'Create a Clue token'; CR 111.10f: Clue is a colorless artifact with '{2}, Sacrifice this token: Draw a card.'). p1 then activates the Clue's ability by paying the remaining {2} generic mana and sacrificing the Clue token (CR 602.2c: sacrifice is a cost paid before the ability is placed on stack). After priority passes, the Clue ability resolves and p1 draws 1 card. Final state: p1 hand size +1 from the draw, Clue token gone, Magnifying Glass still on battlefield (tapped), stack empty.
metadata.generation_notes: Magnifying Glass engine definition (definitions.rs line 2938): ability 0 = '{1},{T}: Add {C}' (AbilityDefinition::Activated with Cost::Sequence([Mana{generic:1}, Tap]), Effect::AddMana); ability 1 = '{3},{T}: Investigate' (AbilityDefinition::Activated with Cost::Sequence([Mana{generic:3}, Tap]), Effect::Investigate). CRITICAL harness behavior: the `enrich_spec_from_def` function in replay_harness.rs only filters out mana abilities when cost is exactly `Cost::Tap` (line 741: `matches!(cost, Cost::Tap)`). Because the first ability has `Cost::Sequence([Mana, Tap])`, it is NOT recognized as a mana ability and is placed in `characteristics.activated_abilities` as index 0. Therefore: ability_index 0 = mana ability '{1},{T}: Add {C}' (incorrectly included); ability_index 1 = Investigate ability '{3},{T}: Investigate'. This means the Investigate ability must use ability_index: 1, not 0. The Clue token (created by Investigate) has ability_index 0 — it has exactly one activated ability, no mana abilities. Mana plan: p1 starts with {5} colorless in mana pool. Activate Magnifying Glass Investigate: pay {3}, tap artifact → 2 colorless remain. Activate Clue: pay {2} + sacrifice → 0 mana remains. Library has one Plains to ensure the draw succeeds. Sacrifice as cost for Clue (CR 602.2c): Clue moves to graveyard at activation time, before ability hits stack. Clue token name is 'Clue' (clue_token_spec name field). Investigate effect creates the token inline during ability resolution (effects/mod.rs line 490: state.add_object, no separate stack entry for token creation). Script 098 (Thraben Inspector Clue) used as reference for the Clue activation pattern.

script.step_note: p1 activates Magnifying Glass's Investigate ability ({3},{T}): pay {3} generic, tap Magnifying Glass

actions.description: Initial: Magnifying Glass on p1's battlefield (untapped), stack empty, hand empty
actions.note: p1 has 5 colorless mana in pool. Magnifying Glass is untapped and ready to activate.

CR 602.2: p1 activates Magnifying Glass ability_index 1 ('{3},{T}: Investigate'). Index 1 because the harness enrich_spec_from_def places the mana ability '{1},{T}: Add {C}' at index 0 (its cost is Cost::Sequence, not filtered as mana-only). Cost: {3} generic paid from pool + tap Magnifying Glass. Magnifying Glass is NOT sacrificed — it stays on battlefield tapped. The Investigate ability is placed on the stack.

actions.description: After Magnifying Glass activation: ability on stack, Magnifying Glass still on battlefield (now tapped)
actions.note: Magnifying Glass remains on battlefield (tapped). Investigate ability is on the stack awaiting resolution. 2 colorless mana remain in p1's pool.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — Investigate ability resolves.

resolution.note: CR 701.16a: Investigate means 'Create a Clue token.' CR 111.10f: Clue token is a colorless Clue artifact with '{2}, Sacrifice this token: Draw a card.' Token enters p1's battlefield (CR 111.1, 111.5).

actions.note: CR 608.2: Investigate ability resolves; Effect::Investigate creates a Clue token directly on the battlefield (no separate stack entry). Magnifying Glass ability goes to the graveyard after resolution.

actions.description: After Investigate resolves: Clue token on p1's battlefield, Magnifying Glass still on battlefield (tapped), stack empty
actions.note: Clue token created on p1's battlefield. Magnifying Glass remains tapped. Hand still empty (no draw yet).

script.step_note: p1 activates the Clue token's ability: pay {2}, sacrifice Clue to draw a card

actions.note: CR 602.2: p1 activates Clue token ability_index 0 ('{2}, Sacrifice this token: Draw a card'). Cost: {2} generic paid from remaining pool + sacrifice Clue token. CR 602.2c: Sacrifice is a cost — Clue moves to graveyard at activation time, before the ability is placed on the stack. No {T} required (CR 111.10f: Clue cost is {2}+Sacrifice only, not {T}). Clue token has exactly one non-mana activated ability, so ability_index 0 is correct.

actions.description: After Clue activation: Clue token sacrificed (in graveyard), ability on stack, hand still empty
actions.note: CR 602.2c: Clue token sacrificed as cost — already in graveyard. Clue draw ability is on the stack. Hand still empty (draw has not resolved yet).
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — Clue draw ability resolves.

resolution.note: CR 701.36: p1 draws 1 card from the top of their library (Plains). Hand size increases from 0 to 1.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Clue activated ability resolves; DrawCards effect executes; p1 draws 1 card.
CR 704.5d / CR 111.7: A token in a zone other than the battlefield ceases to exist as a state-based action. The Clue token was sacrificed as a cost and is in the graveyard.
CR 704.3: SBAs checked whenever a player would receive priority. Clue token in graveyard ceases to exist.

actions.description: Final state: p1 has 1 card in hand, Clue token gone, Magnifying Glass still on battlefield (tapped), stack empty
actions.note: p1 drew the Plains from library (hand 0 → 1). Clue token sacrificed and ceased to exist (CR 704.5d). Magnifying Glass remains on p1's battlefield (tapped). Library is now empty.
